description: Pure Julia port of PyRadiomics with 1:1 unit test parity

overview.goal: Port PyRadiomics to pure Julia with guaranteed parity via exhaustive 1:1 unit testing
overview.approach: Research-first development with PythonCall.jl test harness for validation
overview.testingStrategy: Every PyRadiomics function tested against Julia implementation using deterministic random arrays

phase1.name: Deep Research
phase1.description: Comprehensive research on PyRadiomics architecture, Julia porting patterns, and dependency strategy

phase2.name: Package Setup
phase2.description: Initialize Julia package with proper structure, CI, and test harness

phase3.name: Core Infrastructure
phase3.description: Implement core types, image handling, and mask operations

phase4.name: First Order Features
phase4.description: Implement first-order statistical features with full test coverage

phase6.name: GLCM Features
phase6.description: Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix texture features

phase7.name: GLRLM Features
phase7.description: Gray Level Run Length Matrix features

phase8.name: GLSZM Features
phase8.description: Gray Level Size Zone Matrix features

phase9.name: NGTDM Features
phase9.description: Neighboring Gray Tone Difference Matrix features

phase11.name: Integration & API
phase11.description: High-level API, feature extraction pipeline, and integration tests

userStories.title: Research PyRadiomics Architecture
userStories.description: Deep dive into PyRadiomics codebase to understand overall architecture, module structure, and code organization
acceptanceCriteria: Document PyRadiomics directory structure in progress.md
List all Python modules and their responsibilities
Identify core base classes and inheritance hierarchy
Identify all external dependencies and their purposes
Map out the feature extraction pipeline flow
Document all entry points and public API
Cite specific files and line numbers in PyRadiomics repo

userStories.title: Research All PyRadiomics Feature Classes
userStories.description: Exhaustively document every feature class, every feature function, and their mathematical definitions
acceptanceCriteria: List ALL feature classes (FirstOrder, Shape, GLCM, GLRLM, GLSZM, NGTDM, GLDM)
For EACH class, list EVERY feature function with its mathematical formula
Document the exact function signatures in PyRadiomics
Note any edge cases, special handling, or numerical considerations
Create a complete feature inventory table
Total count of features to implement documented

userStories.title: Research Julia Porting Best Practices
userStories.description: Research how to idiomatically port Python scientific code to Julia, examining successful ports
acceptanceCriteria: Study existing Python-to-Julia ports (e.g., scikit-learn patterns in MLJ)
Document Julian idioms for numpy-style array operations
Research Julia equivalents for scipy functions used in PyRadiomics
Document type system design patterns for feature extraction
Research Julia image processing ecosystem (Images.jl, etc.)
Identify performance optimization opportunities unique to Julia
Document multiple dispatch patterns for feature classes
Research Julia testing best practices (Test.jl patterns)

userStories.title: Research Pure Julia Dependencies
userStories.description: Identify all Julia packages needed and ensure pure-Julia implementation is possible
acceptanceCriteria: Map PyRadiomics dependencies to Julia equivalents
Verify Images.jl can handle required image operations
Verify Statistics.jl / StatsBase.jl cover statistical needs
Research mesh/surface computation packages for shape features
Identify any gaps requiring custom implementation
Document final dependency list with versions
Verify all dependencies are pure Julia (no binary wrappers)

userStories.title: Research and Design Test Strategy
userStories.description: Design comprehensive testing strategy using PythonCall.jl for 1:1 parity verification
acceptanceCriteria: Document PythonCall.jl setup for test environment
Design test harness that installs pyradiomics via CondaPkg
Define random array generation strategy with fixed seeds
Define tolerance thresholds for floating-point comparisons
Design test file organization (one file per feature class)
Document how to run Python feature extraction from Julia tests
Create template test structure for feature parity tests
Document CI strategy for running Python+Julia tests

userStories.title: Initialize Julia Package Structure
userStories.description: Create proper Julia package structure with all required files
acceptanceCriteria: Create Project.toml with metadata and dependencies
Create CondaPkg.toml for pyradiomics in test env
Create basic README.md with project description
Package loads without errors: using Radiomics

userStories.title: Initialize Git Repository
userStories.description: Set up git repository with proper configuration
acceptanceCriteria: Create initial commit with package structure
Set up conventional commit message format
Verify git status shows clean working tree

userStories.title: Set Up GitHub Actions CI
userStories.description: Configure continuous integration for automated testing
acceptanceCriteria: Set up Python/conda for test dependencies

userStories.title: Implement PythonCall Test Harness
userStories.description: Create reusable test utilities for PyRadiomics comparison
acceptanceCriteria: Verify pyradiomics imports successfully in test env

Document test harness usage in test file comments
Commit test harness with descriptive message

acceptanceCriteria: Define RadiomicsImage type or appropriate abstraction
